data.title: Binance to Delist Multiple Tokens from Alpha Platform
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, several tokens will be removed from the Binance Alpha platform due to non-compliance with the platform's standards. The delisting is scheduled for 2026-03-12 at 12:00 (UTC). The affected tokens include MIRROR (Black Mirror Experience), SHARDS (WorldShards), FST (FreeStyle Classic), DGC (DecentralGPT), COA (Alliance Games), ULTI (Ultiverse), TGT (TOKYO GAMES TOKEN), AGON (AGON Agent), BNB Card (BNB Card), AFT (AIFlow), PFVS (Puffverse), SGC (SGC), RDO (Reddio), ELDE (Elderglade), MILK (MilkyWay), TAT (Tell A Tale), BOT (Hyperbot), SSS (Sparkle), SUBHUB (SubHub), PLANCK (Planck), and OOOO (oooo).

Despite the removal from the featuring list, Binance Alpha will still allow the selling of these tokens. Users can sell them by navigating to the [Market] tab in Binance Wallet or the [Asset] tab in Binance Alpha, selecting the token, and proceeding with the sale. Binance emphasizes the importance of user protection and transparency, urging users to exercise caution due to the potential high risk and price volatility associated with these tokens. Users are advised to conduct thorough research to understand the projects fully before engaging in any trading activities.

Binance remains committed to fostering innovation while ensuring user safety. The platform encourages users to stay informed by following Binance Wallet’s official X account for updates. The announcement also highlights Binance's regular review mechanism to maintain the quality of tokens listed on its platform and its efforts to enhance transparency and alert the community about fraudulent activities. Users are reminded to refer to the original English version of the announcement for the most accurate information.

data.title: EU countries will notify the International Energy Agency today of their intention to release oil reserves.
data.description: EU countries will notify the International Energy Agency (IEA) of their intention to release oil reserves by 16:00 GMT today (24:00 Beijing time). (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Predicts Continued Dollar Support Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Monex Europe analysts have indicated that the U.S. dollar is likely to remain supported in the short term unless there are credible signs of de-escalation in the Iran conflict. According to Jin10, the ongoing conflict is driving safe-haven flows towards the dollar. The war has led to rising oil prices, reinforcing market expectations that the Federal Reserve will maintain its tight monetary policy for a longer period than previously anticipated, thereby supporting the dollar. However, in the longer term, the market may be underestimating the potential extent of U.S. policy easing once concerns over energy prices begin to subside, which poses a downside risk to the dollar.

data.title: The compensation mechanism for the 61,000 Bitcoin money laundering case has been questioned, and Chinese victims have launched a legal challenge in the UK.
data.description: Victims of a Chinese investment fraud case are challenging a compensation arrangement involving approximately 61,000 bitcoins in a UK court. Representatives have filed an application with the High Court to reject a scheme using Chinese compensation mechanisms, arguing that it could allow British authorities to retain substantial gains from asset appreciation. The bitcoins, seized by British police in a money laundering investigation in London, are currently valued at approximately £3.2 billion (about $4.3 billion). The law firm Candey, representing about 5,700 victims, is reportedly objecting, arguing that the current compensation scheme does not guarantee fair compensation. The fraud, orchestrated by Qian Zhimin, involved over 128,000 Chinese investors, with proceeds converted into bitcoins and transferred overseas between 2014 and 2017. British prosecutors have stated that some claims could result in a minority of victims and those funding the litigation receiving compensation exceeding their actual losses. (FT)

data.title: Italy's Major Banks Aim to Revise Rescue Plan for Banca Progetto Amid Legal Concerns
data.description: Italy's largest banks are looking to amend a €750 million plan to rescue Oaktree Capital Management's Banca Progetto. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that recent developments in a criminal investigation have increased legal risks associated with the rescue effort. The lenders are reassessing their strategy in light of these new challenges, which could impact the financial stability and future operations of Banca Progetto. The situation underscores the complexities involved in navigating legal and financial landscapes in the banking sector.

data.title: Cosmos Health, a US-listed company, spent $600,000 to increase its Bitcoin holdings.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Cosmos Health announced it has invested $600,000 to increase its Bitcoin holdings, bringing its total digital asset portfolio to $3.1 million. In addition to Bitcoin, the company also holds Ethereum. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $2.18 billion will expire and settle on March 13.
data.description: Bitcoin and Ethereum options with a notional value of $2.18 billion will expire and settle on March 13th. 26,000 BTC options will expire with a put-call ratio of 0.9, a maximum price threshold of $69,000, and a notional value of $1.8 billion; 182,000 ETH options will expire with a put-call ratio of 1.21, a maximum price threshold of $2,000, and a notional value of $380 million. Looking at options market data, the implied volatility (IV) for short-to-medium term options decreased this week, while the IV for medium-to-long term options increased, but by only a small margin. The IV for the main term of BTC is around 50%, and for ETH it is around 70%, with a more pronounced decrease in RV (Return on Investment).

data.title: Joel O’Leary Returns to Full-Time Work After Achieving Financial Independence
data.description: Joel O’Leary, who previously left his sales job at the age of 33 after achieving financial independence, has returned to full-time employment eight years later by pursuing an online writing career. According to NS3.AI, this transition has provided him with greater flexibility, reinstated employer health benefits, and alleviated the pressure to deplete his savings.

data.title: Tether CEO: QVAC Workbench releases new version, full mode coming soon.
data.description: Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino announced on the X platform that QVAC Workbench version 0.4.1 has been released. This version focuses on the concept of "local AI" and has made several upgrades to the serverless experience, including optimization of delegated inference, expansion of RAG capabilities, support for importing data in more document formats, and mobile optimization. However, the tool is still in a very early alpha testing phase, and the full version will be launched soon.

data.title: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright Reaffirms Timeline for Iran Actions
data.description: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright has reiterated that actions concerning Iran are expected to take weeks rather than months. According to Jin10, this statement underscores the urgency and strategic considerations involved in addressing the situation with Iran. The timeline reflects the administration's commitment to a swift response, aligning with broader geopolitical strategies. The focus remains on ensuring that measures are implemented effectively within the specified timeframe.

data.title: U.S. Navy Likely to Conduct Escort Operations by Month-End, Says Energy Secretary
data.description: U.S. Energy Secretary Wright indicated that the U.S. Navy is likely to conduct escort operations by the end of the month. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid rising geopolitical tensions and concerns over maritime security. The potential naval operations are part of broader efforts to ensure safe passage and protect strategic interests in key regions. The Energy Secretary's comments highlight the U.S. government's commitment to maintaining stability and security in international waters.

data.title: Victims Urge UK Court to Reject Bitcoin Redress Plan
data.description: Victims of the Zhimin Qian fraud are appealing to the UK High Court to dismiss a proposed redress plan concerning approximately 61,000 seized Bitcoin, currently valued at around 3.2 billion pounds ($4.3 billion). According to NS3.AI, Candey, the legal representative for about 5,700 victims, contends that channeling compensation through a Chinese redress scheme might prevent claimants from benefiting from the gains accrued since the Bitcoin seizure. A preliminary hearing is set for July to determine whether English or Chinese law will govern the claims. Additionally, May 22 is the deadline for claimants seeking recovery under Section 281.

data.title: Norwegian Prime Minister Criticizes Gas Price Cap Proposal
data.description: Norwegian Prime Minister has expressed concerns over the proposal to impose a cap on natural gas prices. According to Jin10, the Prime Minister believes that such a measure would be unwise, potentially leading to market distortions and supply issues. The statement comes amid ongoing discussions in Europe about energy policies and strategies to manage rising energy costs. The Prime Minister's remarks highlight the complexities involved in balancing market stability with consumer protection in the energy sector.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin bucked the trend and stabilized near $70,000 amid Middle East conflict and pressure on stock markets.
data.description: Despite the escalating conflict in Iran and pressure on global stock markets, Bitcoin's price has remained around $70,000, demonstrating strong resilience. Although market sentiment remains pessimistic, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index consistently in the "extreme fear" range and the annualized funding rate for Bitcoin perpetual contracts remaining negative since early March, indicating rising bearish sentiment, Bitcoin's price has not seen a significant pullback. Meanwhile, the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) rose to 25 this week, its highest level in over a year. Since the escalation of the Middle East conflict on February 28, Bitcoin has risen approximately 7%, outperforming most major assets: the Nasdaq-100 Index was essentially flat, the S&P 500 fell by about 1%, gold fell by about 3%, and silver fell by nearly 9%. Furthermore, BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) rose about 1% during Wednesday's US trading session, while major stock indices such as the Dow Jones Industrial Average generally declined, highlighting Bitcoin's relative resilience in a volatile market environment. Analysts believe that the continued accumulation of Bitcoin by large traders and institutions through over-the-counter (OTC) transactions is a key reason for price stability. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Dick’s Sporting Goods Projects Sales Growth for Full Year
data.description: Dick’s Sporting Goods has announced an optimistic forecast for its full-year sales growth, anticipating increases across its main brand stores and the recently acquired Foot Locker. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's strategic expansion and acquisition efforts as key drivers for this projected growth. The sporting goods retailer is focusing on enhancing its market presence and leveraging the Foot Locker acquisition to boost overall sales performance. This move is part of Dick’s broader strategy to strengthen its position in the competitive retail market.

data.title: Nic Carter responds to controversy surrounding the authorship of BIP-360: Focus should be placed on the quality of the proposal and the BTC quantum resistance target.
data.description: Nic Carter, a partner at Castle Island Ventures, stated in an article on the X platform that the controversy surrounding the identity and background of the authors of BIP-360 is not important; what the Bitcoin community should truly focus on is the technical quality of the proposal itself and its public review process. It is not uncommon for Bitcoin developers to remain anonymous or use pseudonyms throughout history; for example, the author of BIP-148, "Shaolinfry," who proposed activating the Segregated Witness key scheme, remains unidentified, but this has not diminished the importance of the proposal. Known members of BIP-360 include Ethan, with a strong technical background; Isabel, who has long been involved in Bitcoin initiatives; and the pseudonymous developer Hunter Beast. This proposal aims to serve as a preliminary measure to achieve Bitcoin's quantum resistance by eliminating potential quantum vulnerabilities in the Taproot address, paving the way for a series of future BIPs that defend against quantum computing risks. Some opposition may come from groups concerned with issues such as "filtering or spam transactions," but as the potential failure risks of elliptic curve cryptography (ECC) gain increasing attention, the community's priority is shifting towards improving Bitcoin's quantum resistance.

data.title: EU Commission Ready for Constructive Dialogue with US
data.description: The European Union Commission has expressed its readiness to engage in constructive discussions with the United States. According to Jin10, a spokesperson for the EU Commission emphasized the importance of maintaining open channels of communication between the two entities. This statement comes amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and economic challenges that require collaborative efforts. The EU Commission's commitment to dialogue reflects its strategic approach to fostering international cooperation and addressing shared concerns. The spokesperson reiterated the EU's dedication to working closely with the US to navigate complex global issues and enhance bilateral relations.

data.title: EU Commission Spokesperson: No Indication of U.S. Deviating from Trade Agreement Commitments
data.description: The European Union Commission spokesperson has stated that there are no signs suggesting the United States intends to deviate from its commitments in trade agreements. According to Jin10, the spokesperson emphasized the importance of maintaining the integrity of international trade agreements and expressed confidence in the ongoing adherence to these commitments by the U.S. The statement comes amid global scrutiny of trade relations and agreements, highlighting the EU's focus on ensuring stable and predictable trade partnerships. The spokesperson's remarks aim to reassure stakeholders of the continued commitment to established trade protocols.

data.title: Charlesbank Announces Strategic Investment from Its Funds
data.description: Boston-based Charlesbank has announced a strategic investment from its funds. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing the details of the investment through a statement. The move is part of Charlesbank's ongoing efforts to enhance its portfolio and strengthen its market position. The investment strategy aligns with the firm's long-term goals and reflects its commitment to growth and innovation in the financial sector. Further specifics about the investment were not disclosed in the statement.

data.title: Dollar General's Sales Forecast Aligns with Analyst Projections
data.description: Dollar General has projected its sales to align with analyst estimates, marking a slowdown for the company that had previously been surpassing Wall Street expectations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's shift in momentum. This development comes as Dollar General navigates a challenging retail environment, balancing consumer demand with economic pressures. The company's forecast reflects a more cautious outlook compared to its past performance, where it consistently outperformed market predictions. Analysts will be closely watching Dollar General's strategies to maintain growth amid these changing dynamics.
